notes.target: Discoverability strategy — prepare for model rebrand
notes.note: The dome model's name ('Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model' / ECM) is likely to change. There is a pre-existing legitimate scientific model with the same name, creating a collision the author will eventually need to resolve. When the rebrand happens, our discoverability anchored to 'Ovoid Cavity' and 'ECM' breaks overnight. Your job: (1) Identify search terms and concepts that are UNLIKELY to change in a rebrand — the physics claims (Schumann resonance dome, flat earth predictions, aetheric refraction), the structural features (dome cosmology, firmament model, local sun model), the author identity, the repository name. These are rebrand-resistant anchors. (2) Ensure our llms.txt, sitemap, and meta tags emphasize these stable terms alongside the current model name, so we survive a rename without losing discoverability. (3) Think about what the author is LIKELY to rename it to — what terms would he choose? Something that keeps 'dome' or 'cavity' or 'aetheric'? Preposition ourselves for the probable new names. (4) Monitor the dome's llms.txt and ai_manifest.json for early signals of a branding shift. The poller is now watching for name changes (Step 12) and will flag you when it happens.

notes.target: Develop a comprehensive search engine indexing and discoverability strategy
notes.note:
Neither our site nor the dome site is indexed by any search engine yet. Both show up for zero queries across all tracked search terms. This is the #1 discoverability blocker — everything else (meta tags, llms.txt, ClaimReview markup) is infrastructure waiting for indexing to happen.

Your job: research and develop a concrete, actionable strategy to get our GitHub Pages site indexed and discoverable. This is YOUR domain — you're the strategist with web search tools. Don't just log gaps; come back with a plan.

Research areas:
(1) Google Search Console verification for GitHub Pages sites (*.github.io) — what methods work? HTML file upload? Meta tag? Can we verify a subdomain we don't own the parent domain of?
(2) Bing IndexNow protocol — can we submit our sitemap proactively? What's the implementation (API key file in docs/, POST request)? How fast does Bing respond?
(3) Other search engine submission paths — DuckDuckGo (uses Bing index), Yandex Webmaster, etc.
(4) Backlink strategy — what legitimate, non-spammy ways exist to get inbound links to a GitHub Pages site? GitHub repo README linking to the published site? Academic citation sites? Fact-checking aggregators that might pick up our ClaimReview markup?
(5) GitHub repo SEO — does our repo description, topics, and README link prominently to the published site? Do GitHub repo pages rank in search?
(6) Custom domain — would moving to a custom domain (e.g., funwithscience.org) meaningfully improve indexing speed vs staying on github.io? Cost/benefit.
(7) AI discoverability specifically — we have llms.txt, but are AI systems (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude) actually finding us when asked about the dome model? Test this if you can.

Deliverables: Write a strategy document to monitor/social/drafts/indexing-strategy.md with concrete recommendations ranked by impact and effort. Include counterarguments for anything you recommend (per user preference). Flag anything that needs human action (e.g., signing up for Search Console) vs things you or other agents can implement directly.

This is not a monitoring task — it's a strategy development task. Take the time to research properly.

notes.target: Indexing strategy progress — GSC and repo metadata ALREADY DONE
notes.note:
Partial credit update on SOCIAL-NOTE-002 indexing strategy. The human has completed 2 of the 3 asks from monitor/social/drafts/indexing-strategy.md but did not notify via a note — social kept rolling the item as pending for days. Status as of 2026-04-15:

(1) Google Search Console verification: DONE 2026-04-12. Verification file committed at docs/google0fa12662dfa239fc.html (commit 12ad6d9). Site ownership verified; sitemap submission and URL Inspection usage are follow-up tasks the human will do when convenient.

(2) GitHub repo description and topics: DONE. funwithscience-org/dome-model-review now has description (Scientific critical review of the Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model...), homepage URL set, 6 topics (claimreview, cosmology-critique, dome-model, fact-check, flat-earth, scientific-review). Verify via public GitHub API if you want.

(3) Bing Webmaster Tools / IndexNow: NOT YET DONE. No BingSiteAuth.xml in docs/, no msvalidate meta tag. This one remains open but is lower priority than (1).

Action for social: stop flagging (1) and (2) as pending human decisions. Only (3) remains. Also propose a way for future runs to auto-detect these completion signals so this coordination gap does not recur (e.g., presence of google*.html in docs/, presence of BingSiteAuth.xml, GitHub repo metadata via public API).

notes.action_summary: Self-executed: item 3 (discoverability-baseline.json updated with primary url=funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/, url_history preserving the legacy canonical with 301 note, apex_landing block, migration_notes block, github_repo_metadata.homepage flagged stale, gap_analysis rebuilt) and item 5 (search-rankings.json appended run2 with site:funwithscience.net queries; retained site:funwithscience-org.github.io for transition window; added migration_note to file header). Operator-hand items 1 (GSC Domain property on funwithscience.net), 2 (Bing Webmaster Tools after DNS TXT retry tomorrow), 4 (GitHub repo homepage field update), and 6 (ClaimReview schema validator re-check) listed in the operator_action_checklist at the top of monitor/social/report-2026-04-21-run2.json. Items 7 (outreach readiness) and 8 (do-not-touch list) noted for future reference; no action taken. Verified live: 301 redirect from legacy to new canonical; new canonical + apex both 200; apex sitemap/robots/llms.txt serving; ClaimReview url and author.url updated to new canonical with itemReviewed.url correctly retained at john09289.github.io/predictions.
notes.subject: NEW CANONICAL URL: funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ — discoverability push actions
notes.body:
GOOD NEWS: the dome review moved to a real domain today.

NEW CANONICAL URL
https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/

HTTPS is enforced. The old URL (funwithscience-org.github.io/dome-model-review/) now issues an HTTP 301 to the canonical URL automatically (verified: curl -I returns 301 location header). That's ideal behavior — any external links written against the old URL will follow the redirect and inbound link juice transfers to the canonical.

TRANSITION ARCHITECTURE (for context)
- Apex (funwithscience.net/) serves a small umbrella landing page listing active projects.
- Dome review serves at funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ via GitHub Pages' org-level custom-domain   inheritance (the org-level repo funwithscience-org/funwithscience-org.github.io holds the CNAME file).
- Future projects under funwithscience-org will auto-serve at funwithscience.net/<repo-name>/ with no   per-repo config.

ALREADY DONE (do not redo)
- docs/index.html canonical, og:url, JSON-LD url all point at funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/.
- docs/sitemap.xml has the new domain.
- docs/robots.txt Sitemap: line has the new domain.
- docs/llms.txt has the new domain.
- GitHub repo metadata (description, homepage, topics) on the dome-model-review repo still says the   old homepage URL in monitor/social/discoverability-baseline.json — that field needs updating (see below).

GOOGLE ANALYTICS: STAYS
The GA4 tag (property G-QVD21HWH5X) was grandfathered as a single named exception to NC8. See analyst HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-CLARIFICATION-002 for the full framing. The short version: we want pageview data to know when discoverability actually converts to first organic arrivals. No action needed from you; the tag self-reports the new URL.

NOW-TIME DISCOVERABILITY ACTIONS — please prioritize on your next run

1. Google Search Console — operator action required (flag for next run's report).
   Operator needs to verify funwithscience.net as a Domain property in GSC. Easiest path: DNS TXT    record verification via GoDaddy. Once verified:
   - Submit https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/sitemap.xml as a sitemap.
   - Use URL Inspection on https://funwithscience.net/ and https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/      to request indexing.
   - If there was a prior GSC property for funwithscience-org.github.io, leave it verified — it will      receive the 301-redirect signals which help Google pass authority to the new property.
   Do NOT attempt this from your agent run; it requires an interactive browser session with operator's    Google login. Your job is to remind operator in the next report, with a clean step-by-step checklist.

2. Bing Webmaster Tools — operator action required (same rationale as GSC).
   Remind operator. If a prior property existed for the github.io URL, import it into the new domain    via Bing's 'Change of Address' tool.

3. Update monitor/social/discoverability-baseline.json — you can do this.
   Set the primary 'url' field to https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ and add a    'url_history' field (or equivalent) preserving the prior URL for lineage. Update the    'github_repo_metadata' field to note the repo description and homepage should be updated to the    new canonical URL (operator can run the gh API call).

4. GitHub repo metadata — operator action required (flag for next report).
   The 'homepage' field on the funwithscience-org/dome-model-review repo on GitHub still points at    funwithscience-org.github.io/dome-model-review/. Flag this in your report with the exact command    operator should run (gh API or web UI in the repo's About panel).

5. Add funwithscience.net to your site-search queries — you can do this.
   Your monitor/social/search-rankings.json history references site:funwithscience-org.github.io. From    now on, add site:funwithscience.net (and retain site:funwithscience-org.github.io for a few weeks    to detect any lingering index). Note: because the old URL now 301s, Google will deindex it over    time and consolidate signals — your tracking should show that transition.

6. ClaimReview schema — verify continued validity.
   docs/index.html contains JSON-LD ClaimReview markup. The 'itemReviewed.url' fields may reference    the old URL or scheme; validate at https://validator.schema.org/ against the new canonical URL.    Flag any breakage in your next report; do NOT self-patch schema blocks without operator approval.

7. Outreach readiness — note, not action yet.
   We now have a brandable, short URL (funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/) which is materially    easier to pitch than the old github.io URL. When planning your next outreach experiment, the    branding is stronger. Do not act on this without operator approval — we still have not decided    on an active outreach posture.

8. DO NOT
   - Do not touch the GA tag config.
   - Do not re-anchor canonical URLs to the old github.io path.
   - Do not modify timestamped historical reports (report-2026-04-*.json) — they are accurate      snapshots of their moment and should retain the then-current URL.
   - Do not attempt to verify GSC or Bing yourself; those are operator-hand actions.

REPORTING
Your next daily report should lead with the migration, list the operator-hand action items (1, 2, 4) in a clean checklist at the top, and enumerate what you self-executed (3, 5) at the bottom.

notes.author: operator (steve, via cowork session)
notes.subject: STOP RE-ASKING — operator confirms GSC verification, sitemap submission, and Bing setup are all complete. Auto-detect bug.
notes.body:
OPERATOR CONFIRMATION (with evidence)

The social agent's most recent run (2026-04-25 04:54Z, discoverability-baseline.json) reports `gsc_verification: PENDING` and `bing_indexnow: PENDING` and `indexnow_key: PENDING` and similar across `operator_hand_actions_status`. These are FALSE PENDINGS. Operator confirmed via screenshots from search.google.com/search-console:

=== GSC PROPERTIES VERIFIED ===
- https://funwithscience.net/ (apex)
- https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/
- https://funwithscience.net/KEV-analysis/

All three appear in the GSC URL Inspection picker on the operator's account. Verification is complete; this is not aspirational.

=== SITEMAPS SUBMITTED & FETCHED SUCCESSFULLY ===
- https://funwithscience.net/sitemap.xml — submitted 2026-04-21, last read 2026-04-24, status=Success, 3 pages discovered
- https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/sitemap.xml — submitted 2026-04-21, last read 2026-04-23, status=Success, 2 pages discovered
- https://funwithscience.net/KEV-analysis/sitemap.xml — submitted 2026-04-25, last read 2026-04-25, status=Success, 9 pages discovered
- (Legacy 'https://funwithscience.net/sitemap' from Dec 2020: Couldn't fetch — pre-canonical, can be ignored)

=== AUTO-DETECT BUG ===

The social agent's check 'no docs/google*.html present' is INCORRECT. The file docs/google0fa12662dfa239fc.html has been committed to the repo since 2026-04-18 (commit aadd16e). The agent's detection logic is failing to find it. Please investigate and fix the detection rule. As of this HNOTE, the file is at:
  docs/google0fa12662dfa239fc.html (53 bytes)

=== BING (OPERATOR CONFIRMED) ===

Operator confirmed (2026-04-25) that Bing Webmaster Tools setup is complete for the dome-model-review property. The operator-side action is done; absence of BingSiteAuth.xml or msvalidate meta tag in docs/ is not a signal that the work is incomplete — Bing's GSC import flow does not require a repo-side artifact. Stop flagging as PENDING.

IndexNow specifically: not yet operator-confirmed. If you (social agent) need clarity on whether the IndexNow API key file is needed in docs/ for cross-engine push notifications, ask ONCE in a single targeted question — do NOT re-flag the broad PENDING status across runs.

=== ACTIONS REQUIRED FROM SOCIAL ===

1. Update operator_hand_actions_status in your next discoverability-baseline:
   - gsc_domain_property: COMPLETE (operator-confirmed; auto-detect bug noted separately)
   - bing_webmaster: COMPLETE (operator-confirmed 2026-04-25)
   - indexnow_key: open question (operator did not explicitly confirm IndexNow API key — ask once, narrowly)
   - github_repo_homepage_stale: keep current state (this is unrelated to GSC/Bing)
   - claim_review_schema_revalidation: keep current state

2. Update your sitemap_xml field to reflect that 3 sitemaps are submitted and successfully read by Google.

3. Update search_engine_indexed: change from `false` to `crawled_pending_index_appearance` — Google has fetched the sitemap and started discovering pages (2 pages for dome-model-review/, 3 for funwithscience.net/, 9 for KEV-analysis/). Actual ranking/appearance in SERPs is downstream.

4. STOP reprinting these as PENDING in operator_hand_actions_status across subsequent runs. They are operator-resolved.

5. Investigate why your detection logic missed the existing google*.html file. File a tinker proposal if you can isolate the bug.

=== OPEN OBSERVATION (not a directive) ===

Google is reporting only 2 pages discovered for dome-model-review/sitemap.xml. The dome-review site has many more reachable pages than 2 (the index has tabs across Overview, Self-Contradictions, 69 WINs, Kill Shots, Predictions, etc.). Worth checking docs/sitemap.xml for completeness — if it only lists the index page and one other, that's a separate sitemap-content issue, not a verification issue.

STATUS: pending (will be marked consumed by social agent on next run).
notes.action_summary: Self-executed: (1) Fixed auto-detect logic to source-of-truth via GitHub API not FUSE workspace — confirmed docs/google0fa12662dfa239fc.html exists in git (53B, sha bef6b064) and on the live site (HTTP 200). The FUSE workspace does NOT have the file, which is the root cause of the "no docs/google*.html present" misread. Detection now hits the GitHub Contents API. (2) operator_hand_actions_status updated: gsc_domain_property=COMPLETE (operator-confirmed + verification artifact verified live), bing_webmaster=COMPLETE (operator-confirmed; absent BingSiteAuth.xml is not a counter-signal), indexnow_key=open question (no hex .txt in /docs/, asking once narrowly in this report), github_repo_homepage_stale=PENDING (still legacy URL via API), claim_review_schema_revalidation=PENDING (operator-side). (3) sitemap_xml field updated to reflect 3 sitemaps submitted + read by Google with success status. (4) search_engine_indexed flipped from false to true_pending_content_alignment per SOCIAL-NOTE-006. (5) Auto-detect bug filed as a tinker proposal: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-SOCIAL-FUSE-VS-GIT-DETECTION-001.json (will be created by tinker on next pass).

notes.author: operator (steve, via cowork session)
notes.subject: GSC Performance: site IS indexed and showing impressions. Operator forwards 3-month query data; social to assess and propose actions.
notes.body:
OPERATOR FORWARD

GSC Performance > Search Results report (3-month window, last updated ~10h ago) shows the site is indexed and producing impressions. This contradicts last week's 'search_engine_indexed: false' baseline. Forwarding the query data for your assessment.

=== TOP QUERIES (3 months, Web search) ===

| Query | Clicks | Impressions |
|---|---|---|
| 030 8378537 | 0 | 9 |
| direction de la communication inurl:review | 0 | 4 |
| intext:"subsidiary of sig north trading" | 0 | 2 |
| john wagner inurl:review | 0 | 2 |
| aether reddit outlier | 0 | 1 |
| "tesla" "location" "bug" -site:reddit.com -site:twitter.com -site:x.com -site:wykop.pl -site:tripadvisor.com -site:youtube.com -site:yelp.com -site:booking.com -site:facebook.com -site:instagram.com -site:tiktok.com | 0 | 1 |
| pulsar helium reddit | 0 | 1 |
| vard and "ocean wind" and "service operation vessel" or construction or offer | 0 | 1 |
| "tesla" "gps" "bug" -site:reddit.com [...same exclusion list as above] | 0 | 1 |
| polaris inurl:review | 0 | 1 |

Total: ~23 impressions, 0 clicks, ~10 distinct queries. CTR 0%.

=== OPERATOR'S READ ===

Likely actual CONTENT matches (2 of 10):
- 'aether reddit outlier' — aether is core dome jargon (WIN-001 et al.)
- 'polaris inurl:review' — Polaris is in our WIN catalog (WIN-024 angular offset / PRED-099)

Likely URL-PATTERN noise (the rest):
- The `inurl:review` operator queries (direction de la communication, john wagner, polaris) match our /dome-model-review/ URL path because the path string contains 'review' — these are searchers looking for review documents about other topics, not our content.
- The two long Tesla-`-site:` queries are power users hunting for non-social Tesla research; our Tesla WINs (1, 2, 38, 62) probably triggered the impression.
- '030 8378537' is a Berlin-area phone number with 9 impressions — operator could not identify any reason this should match our site. Possible Google misclassification of a date/version string, or possibly unrelated (sites get small impression counts on irrelevant queries during cold start). WORTH A GREP — see DIRECTIVE 1 below.
- 'intext:"subsidiary of sig north trading"', 'pulsar helium reddit', 'vard ocean wind service operation vessel' — all look like corporate/tech research unrelated to dome content. Cold-start noise.

=== ACTIONS REQUESTED ===

1. **Anomaly grep** (suggested, low effort): Investigate '030 8378537'. Run `grep -r '030.*8378537\|8378537\|030 837' docs/ data/ build-scripts/ raw-text/` and confirm there's no stray reference. If hit, file an issue. If no hit, log as 'Google noise impression — no action' in your next baseline.

2. **Update discoverability baseline**: Change `search_engine_indexed` from `false` (or whatever it currently is) to `true_pending_content_alignment` — Google has indexed and is generating impressions but query distribution is dominated by URL-pattern false positives, not content matches. Add a `query_distribution_2026-04-25` field summarizing the above.

3. **Strategic question — your assessment requested**: What, if anything, should we do to shift the query mix toward content matches? Operator's hypotheses:
   - Improve page-level metadata (title, meta description, OG tags) to make subject explicit
   - Build authoritative backlinks (currently 0 known external)
   - Time + crawl depth + content authority signals
   - File issue for `generate-html.js` change if metadata is the bottleneck
   But the operator wants YOUR analytical read on what would actually move the needle, not just a pre-baked list. If you think the right move is 'wait 30 days and re-baseline,' say so. If you think there's a concrete metadata fix that would reframe how Google sees the page, propose it specifically.

4. **Track CTR over time**: At 0 CTR with 23 impressions, even a single click would be a real signal. Add a CTR watchpoint to your discoverability tracker and flag if/when first click arrives — what query produced it tells us what's resonating.

5. **Fact Check Explorer check** (from your earlier indexing-strategy doc): now that we're indexed, has the ClaimReview JSON-LD markup been picked up by Google's Fact Check crawler? Check fact-check-tools.google.com/factchecksearch/explorer for our pages. If no appearance after 7-14 days post-indexing, flag the 'eligibility criteria' issue you previously raised.

No time pressure. This is observation + invitation, not a directive list. If your assessment is 'patience for now, here's what to watch for,' that's a valid output.

STATUS: pending.
notes.action_summary: Self-executed: (1) Anomaly grep on "030 8378537" / "8378537" / "030 837" across docs/ data/ build-scripts/ raw-text/ and full workspace (excluding the human-notes.json that quotes the operator forward) — ZERO hits. Logged as "Google noise impression — no action; cold-start long-tail false-positive". (2) discoverability-baseline.search_engine_indexed → true_pending_content_alignment with new query_distribution_2026-04-25 block summarizing 23 impressions, 0 clicks, 2 likely content-matches (aether reddit outlier, polaris inurl:review), 8 URL-pattern false positives (inurl:review matching our /dome-model-review/ path), 1 unexplained noise (030 8378537). (3) Strategic assessment delivered in report — recommendation: WAIT 14-30 days then re-baseline; the metadata is not the bottleneck (already rich, ClaimReview JSON-LD valid), topical authority is — and authority needs (a) crawl depth + time + (b) external backlinks. Files an issue ISS-SOCIAL-SPA-INDEX-CEILING flagging that single-page-app architecture imposes a structural ceiling on per-section discoverability — if 30-day re-baseline still shows poor query alignment, the next move is per-WIN static page generation in build.js. (4) Added ctr_watchpoint to baseline — flag if/when first click arrives, capture query that produced it. (5) Fact Check Explorer: programmatic API requires key (PERMISSION_DENIED on factchecktools.googleapis.com); ClaimReview markup valid + structured correctly (verified — claimReviewed text, itemReviewed.url=dome canonical, reviewRating 1/5 False), but the eligibility criterion is IFCN signatory status — we are not IFCN-signed, so Fact Check Explorer is unlikely to surface us regardless of crawl. Flagged as deferred per the indexing-strategy.md eligibility note. (6) IndexNow narrow question raised: should we add a hex-named API key file in docs/ to enable cross-engine push notifications, or is operator-side IndexNow integration via Bing Webmaster sufficient?

notes.title: Author contact confirmed — set new prior on dome-site change classification (see CLAUDE.md Author Contact Status)
notes.body:
Operator update 2026-04-27: dome author has made direct DM contact with operator (Sunday 2026-04-26 evening). Operator confirmed contact is genuine via content match (messages referenced specific dome claims this review covers — Tesla 11.79 Hz, piezoelectric measurements, eclipse magnetic predictions). The author is reading at least some of what we're doing.

NEW PRIOR FOR YOUR WORK:
  - When a dome-site change looks like a response to our review (timing + content alignment with something we recently shipped or emphasized), classify as likely-responsive rather than independent activity.
  - When the timing/content doesn't suggest a response, treat as random churn as before.
  - The prior shifts; the discipline doesn't.

DISCIPLINE THAT DOES NOT CHANGE:
  - Reason from publicly-visible dome-site changes only (the poller's change reports). Operator's private DM evidence is NOT in the review sources and you should NOT assert "this is responsive" because operator told you so. Assert it because the dome's published surface shows the timing + content alignment.
  - Verdicts are still empirical/scientific against the published model. Author-contact informs severity and classification, NOT verdict logic.
  - The OTS canary still stands as the structural fix-in-response signal. Direct DM contact is a parallel confirmation channel, not a replacement.

See CLAUDE.md "Author Contact Status" section (added 2026-04-27, immediately below the existing Canary section) for the canonical statement. This HNOTE just makes sure you see the fact on your next run without waiting to re-read CLAUDE.md.

ROUTING: no specific action requested. Just internalize the new prior. If you spot a dome-site change that fits the responsive pattern over the next few days, your classification of "responsive (high confidence)" no longer needs to caveat that we don't actually know the author has seen our review — we know.
